Meet Paz

★ ★ ★

I am a long-time resident of this district and the father of three future MVLA students. My commitment to education leadership began early—from serving as a Scout leader to mentoring students in the sciences and young women through the Built By Girls foundation. Today, I continue that work as a volunteer in my children's school and as a Board Member for Women in CyberSecurity (WiCyS) Silicon Valley, where I focus on reducing barriers and supporting women's success in a competitive field. I hold a degree in computer science and an MBA from the Kelley School of Business at Indiana University.

As an engineer in a fast-changing industry, I have seen firsthand that a strong foundation and adaptability are crucial to navigating constant change. I am running for the Board to bring an analytical, future-focused vision to our district. My priority is to keep our schools academically strong and fiscally accountable, while ensuring robust mental health support for our students and professional growth and work stability for our teachers and staff.

As an immigrant from a humble background and a product of public education, I deeply appreciate the power of education as a path toward social mobility. I am committed to data-informed policies that bridge the achievement gap for socioeconomically disadvantaged students and students from underserved backgrounds, ensuring every student has the tools to reach their full potential.

Leadership begins with listening. We are fortunate to live in a diverse community where students, teachers, and community members bring incredible dedication to our schools. I am committed to listening to all voices and considering different perspectives to ensure our decisions reflect the needs of the entire community. I bring a lifelong passion for education, a parent's stake in our district, and the professional experience to lead.

Our district is strong, and our students are growing up in a world of extraordinary possibilities. I am committed to maintaining organizational stability while preparing them for the road ahead. I would be honored to serve our community on the MVLA High School Board.

★

Strong foundations and adaptability are essential to students' success.

Why I'm Running

Passionate about education and ready to serve.

★ ★ ★

I am running for the MVLA Board because I am deeply passionate about education and its power to shape our children's futures and the strength of our community. As a parent of three future MVLA students, I have a personal stake in our schools' success. I am ready to serve and contribute my knowledge and skills to ensure our district provides a quality education that is both academically rigorous and emotionally supportive.

High school is a transformative time for students to find their passions and develop the resilience they need for the future. In our competitive landscape, I am driven to maintain academic excellence and decrease achievement gaps while prioritizing student mental health. My goal is to represent our diverse community and advocate for the individualized support and safe environments every student needs to thrive.

My Vision for MVLA

Schools where every student can thrive.

Academically rigorous high schools where students graduate prepared for college, careers, and a rapidly changing world.

Campuses where every student feels safe, supported, and respected — with real mental health resources in place.

A district that attracts and retains exceptional educators through support, development, and professional stability.

Transparent, data-informed governance that closes achievement gaps and keeps MVLA financially strong.

Paz's Priorities

My commitments to the district.

01
Priority One
Strong Academic Standards
With individualized support for every student
  • High academic standards with targeted support
  • Proven teaching practices and steady growth
  • Data-informed policies to reduce achievement gaps
02
Priority Two
Student Well-Being
Safe, supportive high school environments
  • Strengthen campus safety and mental health resources
  • Partner with families and community organizations
  • Student well-being as a top district priority
03
Priority Three
Supporting Our Educators
Teachers and staff are our foundation
  • Meaningful professional development and stability
  • Leadership that listens and respects educators
  • When educators thrive, students excel
04
Priority Four
Responsible Governance
Transparent, fiscally sound decision-making
  • Careful financial stewardship and long-term planning
  • Transparent decisions and clear communication
  • Data-driven governance that keeps MVLA strong
